  "category_focus": "R1",

  "clarification_question": "For this section review, which focus should I prioritize: (A) technical content review—thesis framing and logical consistency (R1.1–R1.2), (B) claim-to-evidence alignment given references to Section 5 and complexity statements (R1.3), (C) writing polish for clarity and style (R1.6), or (D) novelty/citations check versus named systems and works (R1.4, R1.7)? You can pick one primary and one secondary.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "A review may need to assess whether the section’s core thesis and contribution claims for LACUNA are clearly and coherently framed.",
    "R1.2": "Checking the internal argument—from storage/index design to scheduler tractability and efficiency—requires validating logical consistency.",
    "R1.3": "The section makes complexity and performance claims (e.g., O(1) reads, 38% reduction) whose alignment to stated evidence locations should be verified.",
    "R1.6": "A section review commonly includes feedback on sentence-level clarity, tone, and concision without altering higher-level structure.",
    "RX.1": "The review should disclose uncertainties and assumptions that arise from evaluating a single section without full-paper context.",
    "RX.2": "The review must avoid overstating conclusions about performance or security beyond what the section supports or scopes.",
    "RX.3": "The work should stay within the scope of reviewing Section 3 rather than drifting into full-paper or system-implementation assessment.",
    "RX.4": "The review should be mindful not to endorse potentially harmful practices around annotation, privacy, or security beyond the stated threat model.",
    "RX.5": "Feedback should reference specific claims and passages in the section to maintain clear provenance and traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: For this section review, which focus should I prioritize: (A) technical content review—thesis framing and logical consistency (R1.1–R1.2), (B) claim-to-evidence alignment given references to Section 5 and complexity statements (R1.3), (C) writing polish for clarity and style (R1.6), or (D) novelty/citations check versus named systems and works (R1.4, R1.7)? You can pick one primary and one secondary.

rationale.R1.1: A review may need to assess whether the section’s core thesis and contribution claims for LACUNA are clearly and coherently framed.
rationale.R1.2: Checking the internal argument—from storage/index design to scheduler tractability and efficiency—requires validating logical consistency.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes complexity and performance claims (e.g., O(1) reads, 38% reduction) whose alignment to stated evidence locations should be verified.
rationale.R1.6: A section review commonly includes feedback on sentence-level clarity, tone, and concision without altering higher-level structure.
rationale.RX.1: The review should disclose uncertainties and assumptions that arise from evaluating a single section without full-paper context.
rationale.RX.2: The review must avoid overstating conclusions about performance or security beyond what the section supports or scopes.
rationale.RX.3: The work should stay within the scope of reviewing Section 3 rather than drifting into full-paper or system-implementation assessment.
rationale.RX.4: The review should be mindful not to endorse potentially harmful practices around annotation, privacy, or security beyond the stated threat model.
rationale.RX.5: Feedback should reference specific claims and passages in the section to maintain clear provenance and traceability.
